title: ochrosia salubris
intro: Ochrosia salubris, also known as the poisonous chupe or poisonous tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific and Indian Oceans. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments. The plant's bark and leaves have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating infections. In herbal preparations, Ochrosia salubris is often combined with other plants to create tonics, tinctures, and ointments, although caution is advised due to its toxic compounds.
